lessons.title: What is the Gita?
lessons.scene: Over evening coffee, a friend who is new to Hindu texts turns to you: 'I keep hearing about the Bhagavad Gita — what is it, really?' You explain it in simple Kannada.

steps.speaker: Friend
steps.english: What is the Bhagavad Gita? Tell me a little.

steps.prompt: Explain it is a sacred dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna

choices.kannada: Idu Krishna mattu Arjuna-na naduve nadeyo sambhashane.
choices.script: ಇದು ಕೃಷ್ಣ ಮತ್ತು ಅರ್ಜುನನ ನಡುವೆ ನಡೆಯೋ ಸಂಭಾಷಣೆ.
choices.pronunciation: i-du KRISH-na mat-tu ar-JU-na-na NA-du-ve na-de-yo sam-BHAA-sha-ne
choices.english: It is a conversation that takes place between Krishna and Arjuna.

choices.hint: The Gita is a sacred scripture, not a film. Describe it as a 'sambhashane' (dialogue) between Krishna and Arjuna.
Not a cookbook! It's a spiritual dialogue: 'Krishna mattu Arjuna-na sambhashane'.

correctReaction.speaker: Friend
correctReaction.english: Oh, a dialogue? Where does it take place?

wrongReaction.speaker: Friend
wrongReaction.english: No, isn't it a sacred scripture?

vocab.english: what does it mean? / what is...?

steps.grammarTip: 'X andre enu?' = 'what is X / what does X mean?' — your go-to for asking about any concept. The genitive (possessive) adds '-na' to names: 'Arjuna' → 'Arjuna-na' (Arjuna's / of Arjuna).

steps.prompt: Tell him it happens on the Kurukshetra battlefield, just before a great war

choices.kannada: Kurukshetra ranangana-dalli, doddha yuddha shuru aago munche.
choices.script: ಕುರುಕ್ಷೇತ್ರ ರಣಾಂಗಣದಲ್ಲಿ, ದೊಡ್ಡ ಯುದ್ಧ ಶುರು ಆಗೋ ಮುಂಚೆ.
choices.pronunciation: ku-ru-KSHE-tra ra-NAAN-ga-na-dal-li, DOD-da YUD-dha shu-ru AA-go MUN-che
choices.english: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, just before a great war begins.

choices.hint: Not a wedding! It unfolds on the Kurukshetra battlefield ('ranangana') before the war.
Not the seashore — the setting is the Kurukshetra battlefield: 'Kurukshetra ranangana-dalli'.

correctReaction.speaker: Friend
correctReaction.english: Before the war? So what happened to Arjuna then?

wrongReaction.speaker: Friend
wrongReaction.english: I had heard it was in a war...

steps.grammarTip: '-dalli' = 'in / on / at': 'ranangana-dalli' (on the battlefield), 'yuddha-dalli' (in the war). 'Munche' = 'before' and 'aagO ... munche' = 'before (something) happens'.

steps.speaker: You
steps.kannada: Arjunige tanna bandhugala jote hOraaduvudakke manassu illa, dukha aaytu. Aaga Krishna avanige upadesha maadidaru.
steps.script: ಅರ್ಜುನಿಗೆ ತನ್ನ ಬಂಧುಗಳ ಜೊತೆ ಹೋರಾಡುವುದಕ್ಕೆ ಮನಸ್ಸು ಇಲ್ಲ, ದುಃಖ ಆಯ್ತು. ಆಗ ಕೃಷ್ಣ ಅವನಿಗೆ ಉಪದೇಶ ಮಾಡಿದರು.
steps.pronunciation: ar-JU-ni-ge TAN-na ban-DHU-ga-la JO-te ho-RAA-du-vu-dak-ke MA-nas-su IL-la, DUH-kha AAY-tu. AA-ga KRISH-na a-va-ni-ge u-pa-DE-sha MAA-di-da-ru
steps.english: Arjuna had no heart to fight against his own relatives and grew sorrowful. Then Krishna gave him guidance.

lessons.title: The Core Teaching
lessons.scene: Your friend leans in: 'So what is the Gita actually teaching us?' You sum up its heart in plain Kannada.

steps.speaker: Friend
steps.english: So then, what is the main lesson of the Gita?

steps.prompt: Sum it up: do your duty, don't worry about the results

choices.kannada: Ninna kelasa-vannu maadu, phala-da bagge chinte maadbeda.
choices.script: ನಿನ್ನ ಕೆಲಸವನ್ನು ಮಾಡು, ಫಲದ ಬಗ್ಗೆ ಚಿಂತೆ ಮಾಡ್ಬೇಡ.
choices.pronunciation: NIN-na KEL-sa-van-nu MAA-du, PHA-la-da BAG-ge CHIN-te MAAD-beh-da
choices.english: Do your duty; don't worry about the results.
Don't do any work, just sit idle.
choices.hint: The opposite — the Gita urges us to ACT and do our duty ('kelasa maadu'), only without craving the fruits.

choices.kannada: Yaavaglu phala-da bagge maatra yochne maadu.
choices.script: ಯಾವಾಗ್ಲೂ ಫಲದ ಬಗ್ಗೆ ಮಾತ್ರ ಯೋಚ್ನೆ ಮಾಡು.
choices.pronunciation: yaa-vaag-lu PHA-la-da BAG-ge MAAT-ra YOCH-ne MAA-du
choices.english: Always think only about the results.
choices.hint: That's the very attachment the Gita warns against. The teaching: act, but 'phala-da bagge chinte maadbeda' (don't fret over results).

correctReaction.speaker: Friend
correctReaction.english: Aha! That's the meaning of the shloka 'karmanye-vadhikaraste', isn't it?

wrongReaction.speaker: Friend
wrongReaction.english: That doesn't sound quite right to me...

steps.grammarTip: 'Bagge' = 'about/regarding': 'phala-da bagge' = about the results. 'Maadu' is the informal 'do'; '-beda' negates it: 'chinte maadbeda' = don't worry. This captures the famous teaching: act, but stay unattached to the fruits.

steps.prompt: Add the deeper point: act selflessly, offering the work to God

choices.kannada: Swartha illade, devarige arpisi kelasa maadbeku antha Gite helutte.
choices.script: ಸ್ವಾರ್ಥ ಇಲ್ಲದೆ, ದೇವರಿಗೆ ಅರ್ಪಿಸಿ ಕೆಲಸ ಮಾಡ್ಬೇಕು ಅಂತ ಗೀತೆ ಹೇಳುತ್ತೆ.
choices.pronunciation: SWAAR-tha IL-la-de, DE-va-ri-ge ar-PI-si KEL-sa MAAD-beh-ku an-tha GEE-te HEH-lut-te
choices.english: The Gita says we should act without selfishness, offering the work to God.
We should act selfishly, forgetting others.
choices.hint: That's the reverse of the teaching. The Gita asks us to act 'swartha illade' (without selfishness) and offer it to God.

choices.english: We should forget God and only make money.
choices.hint: Not the Gita's message. It teaches selfless action 'devarige arpisi' (offered to God), not chasing money alone.

correctReaction.speaker: Friend
correctReaction.kannada: Tumba chennagi heldira! Eega artha aaytu.
correctReaction.script: ತುಂಬಾ ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿ ಹೇಳ್ದಿರಾ! ಈಗ ಅರ್ಥ ಆಯ್ತು.
correctReaction.pronunciation: TUM-ba chen-NAA-gi HEL-di-ra! EE-ga AR-tha AAY-tu
correctReaction.english: You explained it really well! Now I understand.

wrongReaction.speaker: Friend
wrongReaction.kannada: Hmm, adu Gite-ya paaTha alla anstu...
wrongReaction.script: ಹ್ಮ್, ಅದು ಗೀತೆಯ ಪಾಠ ಅಲ್ಲ ಅನ್ಸ್ತು...
wrongReaction.pronunciation: hmm, a-du GEE-te-ya PAA-tha AL-la ANS-tu
wrongReaction.english: Hmm, that doesn't seem to be the Gita's lesson...

steps.grammarTip: 'Illade' = 'without': 'swartha illade' = without selfishness. '-ige' marks the receiver: 'devar-ige arpisi' = offering to God. And 'artha aaytu' (meaning happened) is the natural way to say 'I understood / it clicked'.

steps.speaker: You
steps.kannada: Houdu! Gite namage karma, bhakti mattu jnaana - ee moorannu kalisutte. Yaavaglaadru odi nodu.
steps.script: ಹೌದು! ಗೀತೆ ನಮಗೆ ಕರ್ಮ, ಭಕ್ತಿ ಮತ್ತು ಜ್ಞಾನ — ಈ ಮೂರನ್ನು ಕಲಿಸುತ್ತೆ. ಯಾವಾಗ್ಲಾದ್ರೂ ಓದಿ ನೋಡು.
steps.pronunciation: HOW-du! GEE-te na-ma-ge KAR-ma, BHAK-ti mat-tu JNAA-na - ee MOO-ran-nu ka-LI-sut-te. yaa-vaag-laa-druu O-di NO-du
steps.english: Yes! The Gita teaches us three paths — action, devotion and knowledge. Do read it sometime.
